reasoning:
The baseline 360-day average is 4.83 strategic developments per month, heavily inflated by mid-to-late 2024 events (massive trash balloon campaigns, troop deployments to Russia, and heightened inter-Korean tensions). Since April/May 2025 have been remarkably quiet by contrast (only one major ballistic missile test noted on May 8, the first in two months), the current incidence rate is well below the 2024 average. To resolve affirmatively, the event count in the 30-day target periods must be 5 or more (i.e. strictly greater than ~4.83). 

For the nearest resolution date (2025-06-01, covering May 2 to May 31), only one confirmed test has happened to date, making it highly unlikely to see 4+ more events in the remaining 6 days, meriting a low probability (~15%). 
For 2025-06-24 (May 25 to Jun 23), starting fresh, the subdued current landscape suggests low activity (~24%). 
Looking towards 2025-08-23 (covering late July to late August), the probability rises noticeably to ~38% because this period historically coincides with major US-ROK joint military drills (e.g., Ulchi Freedom Shield), which predictably provoke intense North Korean countermeasures and strategic posturing.
For the farthest horizon (2025-11-21), we face maximal uncertainty, including the potential for high-profile Trump-Kim diplomacy or renewed tensions, thus pulling the forecast closer to the ~50% base rate.
